item.title: Found another one in the wild, just before destruction...
item.description: This is an interesting permutation of our latest bot problem. First it shows a picture with viral possibility, then a completely unrelated and completely made up conspiracy story about AI controlling the world, followed by yet another @ trap link. I tried to go back and get a less shitlordy crop job (I think it was at ~33 points?), but it had already been nuked. User had no comments or favorites, and this was their first post. Posting this for timeline documentation and awareness.
item.dc:creator: TheSecondPiewackit

item.title: Memes/tomfoolery
item.description:
When I was in kindergarten my much older cousin asked what my favorite color was. I'll never forget how visible troubled he looked when I said "I don't know, orange?"
I don't get it, but my s/o said it's unsettling.
I have a cooling pillow and a ceiling fan that came off a Louisiana swamp boat.
"you're the dog now, man!"
-there is no courage without fear -chicks dig scars -never be too proud to talk yourself out of making a huge mistake
Mine's a watermelon, always has been!
Paper airplane contest?

item.title: The Best America - Couple helps stranded Korean tourists during deadly snowstorm. Gets invited by Korean government for a thank you visit.
item.description: A group of South Korean tourists became stranded in Buffalo, New York, during a heavy blizzard on their way to Niagara Falls. Their van got stuck in the snow, so two of them knocked on the door of a nearby house to ask for shovels to dig the vehicle out of a ditch. That was the home of Alexander Campagna and his wife Andrea, who had planned for a quiet Christmas weekend indoors, with a stocked fridge to wait out the snowstorm. The couple invited the nine travelers plus the driver in as house guests, and they stayed for two nights and three days sharing stories, cooking Korean food and watching American football. Alex recalled how the Christmas with the Korean guests began. “We’ve had snowstorms, but not to that extent. … We knew we were going to be stuck at home for many days. So when we heard the knock on our door, we wondered who would be outside in this weather,” he said. At the door were Scott Park and the tour guide asking if they could borrow shovels, but Campagna thought that would have been “like taking a spoon to take water out of the ocean.” Their clothes were already soaked with snow, so the couple asked the travelers to come in, made tea and coffee, and gave them warm socks and blankets. “I got the impression that for the first couple hours, our guests thought they’ll have tea and leave. But it was impossible to get anywhere in that weather, and it was going to be time for dinner soon,” he said. Hoping to bond, he showed the guests Korean food ingredients they had, as the couple loved Korean food, and offered the guests to feel free to have or cook anything they like. “We were surprised to see all the Korean condiments from soy sauce and mirin to chile powder and chile paste. There was even a rice cooker and Korean cookbooks as if they had prepared all this for us,” said Park, who joined the interview. “They made us really feel at home. After our memorable time together, I thought I should do good deeds for others too.” Park’s wife cooked Korean dishes like jeyuk bokkeum, spicy marinated and stir-fried pork, and dakdori tang, a spicy chicken stew, which they all shared together. “They endured the horrible storm with us. When you spend time together in a disaster situation, you feel very bonded,” Andrea said. “By Christmas morning, people were worried and there was this somber feeling. … When the plowers started coming, people were cheering and crying.” https://www.koreaherald.com/article/3125596 As a result, the Korea Tourism Organization offered the couple an all-expense paid trip of 10 days. It also doubled as an opportunity for the organization to promote tourism in the country. Moreover, the couple was provided with the opportunity to experience authentic Korean culture and enjoy the nation's natural beauty. The couple reportedly got the chance to visit the 14th-century Gyeongbokgung Palace and walk through Gwanghwamun Square. They were treated as esteemed guests of the country. Throughout their trip, the couple dined at Michelin-recommended restaurants and were also given private tours of various tourist spots.

item.dc:creator: BiNorwayBoy

item.title: How I quit my job on the 4th.
item.description: Cracker Barrel for anyone wondering, retail sales. And yes that’s the group chat on WhatsApp that was “highly encouraged.” Also yes, they really did give us a pizza party, along with other placations (to the sum of 2 cups, 1 sweater, 2 sandwiches, and 1 plate of spaghetti in 19 months). Also the manager wanted everyone to lie to customers who needed to return something by saying the system was down, because she didn’t want her sales number to go down. There was so much more, but I’m done with this rant to the void. I hate this timeline.
item.dc:creator: MooQueen

item.title: It's a great big universe and we're all really puny..
item.description:
Frank is having some supervised freedom to explore.
But the mirror really confused him. Here are two short videos showcasing his complete lack of comprehension. And some impressive parkour skills.
Chessie and Puddles have made themselves scarce except for one sighting.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Good morning
item.description: Ouch. So, Frank was meowing last night, so I came into the bathroom to sit with him. Long story short, I feel asleep. On the bathroom floor. Like I was 24 again, sleeping off an unfortunate choice of Irish car bombs with a Goldschläger chaser. Uh...Tiny octopus is NOT 24 anymore. Everything hurts. But look at that little face. Going to take some advil and get some sleep in my own bed.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Phone-clearing dump 318
item.description:
Even In Arcadia
Calm down Satan
Pretty sure this was about Sleep Token, my lovely eepy bois.
Oh. How to translate this. Umm so these figures at the pedestrian crossings are called ‘Mrs and Mr Walkman’. The joke is ‘what age is Mrs and Mr Walkman? They’re in the transit age’…. That’s s bit of a crude translation tho… Övergångsställe is a pedestrian crossing and övergångsåldern is menopause.

item.title: It'd still cost more to extract it and send it down to earth.
item.description: Better to keep it where it is and use that resource for space-based manufacturing. Can you imagine how useful gold would be in making electronics in microgravity?
item.dc:creator: Kyzyl

item.title: Sunday is a day of rest - A day we all love the best - No work to do, no boss to impress - Just laze around in your Sunday dress.
item.description:
The morning starts with a cup of tea, Sitting on the couch, feeling carefree, Binge-watching shows on TV, That’s how Sundays are meant to be.
The breakfast is always grand, With pancakes, waffles and toast at hand, Eggs, bacon, and sausages too, All this food just for me and you.
Then we plan to do something fun, Like a walk in the park under the sun, Or maybe a movie, or a game to play, Whatever we do, it’s a Sunday Funday.
But sometimes we’re too lazy to go out, We’d rather stay in, there’s no doubt, Reading books or playing board games, That’s how we spend our Sundays without any shames.
And then comes the afternoon, We’re full from lunch, feeling immune, To any kind of work or chores, Our Sunday relaxation is what we adore.
So we take a nap, or watch a nap-themed show, And dream about places we’d like to go, Maybe the beach or the mountains high, On Sunday, we can dream and fly.
But before we know it, it’s evening time, And we start to feel a little prime, The weekend’s almost over, the work week looms, But we don’t want to leave our Sunday rooms.
And then it’s time to hit the hay, To end our lazy, relaxing day. SOURCE: https://www.instagram.com/t_oo_r_oo/ POEM: "Sunday Poem" by Wayne Anderson
item.dc:creator: stardustLUST

item.title: 14 normal memes
item.description: Hey, did you know my sole reason for meme posting is to get you to listen to my band? Help a shameless musician out by listening to the songs on mute 2-3000 times while doing the laundry. https://open.spotify.com/artist/19ptBf1ldxSQLo60aDiso7?si=P1bBQOm7SDGGyztTkuqEuw What kind of music is it you ask? Standar Norwegian rock. Best regards - me

item.title: Astrid S. sing the Norwegian national anthem <3
item.description: As a Norwegian myself, made me tear up. Absolutely wonderful rendition. Source: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/IV9TqWc56gQ Translation: Yes, we love this country as it rises forth, rugged, weathered, over the water, with the thousands of homes, — love, love it and think of our father and mother //and the saga-night that lays, lays dreams upon our earth.// Lyrics not sung here: https://www.classicfm.com/discover-music/periods-genres/national-anthems/norway-history-english-lyrics/ EDIT: It is the original song, not majorly changed in any way, just a beautiful rendition of it. We appreciate peace, calm and harmony in Norway. So much so, that our Constitution Day consists of children having parades, not military, no display of power, it's entirely a day of coming together and peace.
item.dc:creator: RickRollEditor

item.title: Cat's Uncanny Valley
item.dc:creator: postrediori

item.title: And home...
item.description:
Chessie was happy to see me
Puddles knew something was up
And as far as first introductions go, this wasn't that bad. We'll segregate for a couple of days. Frank gets the bathroom and they can sniff each other through the door. Then we'll see. But we made it. And I'm exhausted. A little more work to do yet though. But good night, and thank you for all the well wishes and support.
Frank would like out now. Yes buddy.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten
